title: Present Tense
description: Master the two key present patterns: 'is/am/are' and the continuous '-raha/rahi hoon'.
culturalNote: In Delhi Hindi, people constantly mix tenses in a single sentence — 'Main ja rahi hoon, tum aao' (I'm going, you come). Understanding present tense forms lets you follow these fast conversations and jump in naturally.

exercises.note: 'Hoon' = I am. 'Main theek hoon' = I'm fine. 'Main yahan hoon' = I'm here. Always pairs with 'main' (I). This is the first-person singular form of 'hona' (to be).

exercises.english: Is / He is / She is / It is
exercises.note: 'Hai' = is (third person singular). 'Woh theek hai' = she/he is fine. 'Khana taiyaar hai' = food is ready. 'Kya yeh sahi hai?' = is this correct? The most used word in Hindi conversations.
'Hain' = are (for plural or formal). 'Aap kaise hain?' = how are you (formal)? 'Woh log theek hain' = those people are fine. Also used formally to a single person: 'Aap bahut smart hain.'

exercises.prompt: Your friend asks 'Kya tum theek ___?' What fills the blank when talking to one friend?

exercises.note: Continuous tense: verb root + rahi (fem) + hoon. Masculine: 'ja raha hoon'. 'Main office ja rahi hoon' = I'm going to the office. 'Kahan ja rahi ho?' = where are you going? Essential pattern — learn it cold.
'Kha rahi hoon' = I'm eating right now. 'Kya kha rahi ho?' = what are you eating? Masculine: 'kha raha hoon'. Any verb: replace 'kha' with verb root + rahi/raha + hoon/hai.
'Dekh rahi hoon' = I'm watching/looking. 'Main Netflix dekh rahi hoon' = I'm watching Netflix. 'Kya dekh rahi ho?' = what are you watching? 'Dekho' = look (command).

exercises.sentence: Main abhi gym _____. Baad mein milte hain.
exercises.sentenceEnglish: I'm going to the gym right now. Let's meet later.

exercises.note: 'Main sach bol rahi hoon' = I'm telling the truth. 'Woh kya bol rahi hai?' = what is she saying? 'Bolte bolte' = while saying/talking. Pair with 'sach' (truth) or 'jhooth' (lie).
'Main soch rahi hoon' = I'm thinking. 'Kya soch rahi ho?' = what are you thinking? 'Sochne do' = let me think. 'Bahut soch rahi hoon aaj kal' = I've been thinking a lot these days.

exercises.prompt: You want to say 'She is working right now.' Which is correct?
exercises.hint: She = woh, feminine continuous = rahi, she is = hai

exercises.instruction: Match the subject to its 'to be' form

exercises.prompt: 'I'm not feeling well right now.' What's the most natural way to say this?
